title: lobelia repanda
intro: Lobelia repanda, also known as Indian tobacco or Kanjikonna, is a flowering plant native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Lobelia repanda include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce pain and inflammation. Lobelia repanda is often used in herbal preparations like teas, tinctures, and infusions, where it is combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
